data_source: 11 completed match scorecards (04/18–07/11/2026) + season aggregate stats
match_approach: Sinclair Lions are Group B's #9 side — 5W/6L, 24 pts — not a walkover. They've beaten five teams this season and will be motivated after WW's high-profile win streak. This is a must-compete swing game: treat it like a must-win. Win the toss and BAT FIRST — post 140+ to give WW's bowling attack a meaningful target. Then bowl them out under 90 for a bonus-point win. Siddhant Kumar should be back in the attack after sitting out Jersey Aces. Don't rotate the lineup excessively: field the strongest available XI.
form_notes: WW have won 6 consecutive matches (Princeton Walk, Plainsboro Hunters, MKCC Weekenders, PRO SQUAD, Troy Titans, Jersey Aces). The Jersey Aces demolition was clinical: bowled out for 68 in 15 overs (Pradhan, Yuvraj, Rathi, Tyagi, Amanraj all shared wickets), then chased 69 in 5.4 overs. Vinit Keni's 34(14) at SR 242 was the fastest innings of the season — he is in career-best form. Amanraj Gupta took a wicket bowling (2-0-6-1) and continues to be the clutch finisher with the bat. Pradhan had an unusual expensive spell vs Jersey Aces (6.00 econ) — his first in 11 games — but still took 3 wickets; his season numbers remain elite (24 wkts, 3.68 econ).

opponent_recent_form.analysis: Sinclair Lions sit in the bottom half of Group B but are not a pushover — they have won 5 games this season including against some mid-table sides. Their NRR is barely positive (+0.1877), which means their wins have been close and their losses heavy. They've gone 5W/6L from 5W/5L, suggesting they lost their latest game. Treat this as a genuine test: WW cannot show up expecting another Jersey Aces walkover. Their batting has shown enough firepower to punish loose bowling, so Pradhan and Yuvraj must be sharp from ball one.

batting_order.name: Vedant Kumar
batting_order.scorecard_form: 22(18), 13(26), 21(18), 0(6), 27(25), 5(8), 2(4), 7(13), DNB in G10, absent G11 (Jersey Aces) — consistent first-choice opener when available
batting_order.reason: The permanent opener when fit. Three 20+ scores in 10 outings provide the early platform. Absent for Jersey Aces (possibly rested or unavailable) — if he plays vs Sinclair Lions, he goes straight back to #1.

batting_order.name: Yogen Phanse
batting_order.reason: Opens or bats #2 depending on lineup. His 17(13) vs Jersey Aces in the powerplay was exactly what was needed. Explosive SR (130–158 in his best outings) creates quick momentum. Keep him early so his power-hitting overlaps with the first few overs.

batting_order.name: Vinit Keni
batting_order.reason: The cornerstone and form batter of the season. His 34(14) at SR 243 vs Jersey Aces — 4 fours and 2 sixes — is the fastest innings WW have had all year. 208 runs at avg 29.71, SR 116 in 11 games. When Keni bats 20+ balls, WW win. Build the innings around him at #3.

batting_order.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
batting_order.scorecard_form: 3, 6, 5, 3, 20(19) Retired Hurt, 12(15), 2, 14(16), 15(17), DNB vs Jersey Aces (chased in 5.4 overs) — useful contributions at 4–6
batting_order.reason: Vice-captain and #1 bowler. Bats 4–6 depending on game state. His pattern of 12–20 run contributions when he faces deliveries is valuable. Didn't bat vs Jersey Aces — game was over too quickly.

batting_order.name: Anurag Dabee
batting_order.reason: Steady contributor who finishes games. Four consecutive not-outs (PRO SQUAD, Troy Titans, Jersey Aces, and MKCC). Good between the wickets, reliable under pressure. Bats at #5 to preserve the middle order.

batting_order.name: Vivek Singh
batting_order.scorecard_form: EARLY ORDER (pos 3–4): 5,0,5,0,0,1 (6 innings). LOWER ORDER (pos 6–7): 11*(14), 52*(27) — 192 SR, 18(14). vs Jersey Aces: 0*(0) (game won before he needed to bat).
batting_order.reason: CRITICAL: Vivek fails at top 4 (11 runs in 6 innings) and wins games from #6–7 (81 runs in 3 innings incl 52* off 27 balls). Float him to #5 only if WW are 50+ in 8 overs with wickets intact. Otherwise hold firm at 6–7. Do NOT promote him just because Sinclair are the opposition.

batting_order.name: Amanraj Gupta
batting_order.scorecard_form: 3(3), 3(10), 2(2) runout, 1(6), 19*(11) SR=173 vs Troy Titans, 13*(15) vs MKCC, 5(3) at #3 vs Jersey Aces
batting_order.reason: Back-to-back match-finishing performances + a wicket bowling vs Jersey Aces (2-0-6-1, econ 3.00). His utility is growing: 45 runs and now 2 wickets this season. Bat him 7th to guarantee balls in big innings; use him as a bowling option if needed.

batting_order.name: Malav Shah
batting_order.scorecard_form: 29(15) 4×6 SR=193 vs JC Warriors; 0(4); 10*(6) SR=167; 1(4); DNB vs PRO SQUAD; DNB vs Troy; DNB vs Jersey Aces
batting_order.reason: The sixer machine at the death. Three consecutive DNBs — WW are winning games before he's needed. When he does bat in close games, he's destructive. Keep him at 8 and hope WW are building a big total that requires his late hitting.

batting_order.name: Siddhant Kumar
batting_order.scorecard_form: 0(1), 2(4), 9(11), 0(4), 3(7), 14*(9) SR=155 vs MKCC; absent/rested vs Jersey Aces
batting_order.reason: After his 4-wkt haul vs Troy Titans and 1-wkt vs MKCC, he sat out Jersey Aces — likely rested. Expect him back vs Sinclair Lions and he should get 3–4 overs. Bats low to protect his bowling quota.

batting_order.name: Yuvraj Rajpurohit
batting_order.reason: 16 wickets (merged Fnu + Yuvraj profiles) at 4.19 economy across the season. Took 3 wickets vs Jersey Aces (4-0-16-3, econ 4.00) with 5 wides — captain must watch his line on the day. Bats low to preserve full bowling quota.

batting_order.name: Abhinav Tyagi
batting_order.reason: Pure bowling asset who picked up a wicket vs Jersey Aces cheaply. Bats tail with licence to swing hard. 8 wickets season at 6.15 econ — raw pace creates surprises.

bowling_rotation.powerplay_note: PowerPlay = 4 overs. Captain can take it ANYTIME between overs 1–8 — this is a strategic weapon, not a fixed block. Best used when opposition's best batsmen are in to force errors under fielding restrictions.

bowlers.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
bowlers.match_data: Season: 24 wkts, econ 3.68. Last 3: 6.00 vs Jersey Aces (3wkts), 1.91 vs Troy (4wkts), 4.25 vs PRO SQUAD (1wkt)
bowlers.reason: WW's best bowler. 24 wickets at 3.68 economy across 11 games — the most dominant individual stat in WW's lineup. His Jersey Aces spell was unusually expensive (6.00) but he still took 3 wickets. Opens the attack and always finishes his 4-over quota.

bowlers.name: Namit Rathi
bowlers.match_data: Season: 5 wkts, econ 2.75. vs Jersey Aces: 2-0-5-1 (econ 2.50). vs Princeton Walk: 3-1-3-2 (econ 1.00).
bowlers.reason: The best economy bowler in the squad (2.75 season avg). His Jersey Aces spell (2.50 econ, 1 wkt) continued his excellence. Use him in the PowerPlay every game he is available.

powerplay.alternative: If Namit Rathi is not playing, use Siddhant Kumar for a PowerPlay burst — his Troy Titans spell (4-0-12-4, econ 3.00) shows he can strike early.

bowlers.name: Malav Shah
bowlers.match_data: Season: 4 wkts, econ 4.05. DNB bowling vs Jersey Aces (not needed). Last 4 full outings: 4.00, 3.75, 3.67, 4.25, 4.00 econ.
bowlers.reason: The containment workhorse. Economy between 3.67–4.25 in every recent outing — concedes under run-a-ball through the middle. His wickets are a bonus; his value is dot-ball pressure that forces errors for others.

bowlers.name: Yuvraj Rajpurohit
bowlers.match_data: Season (merged): 16 wkts, econ 4.19. Last 3: 4-0-16-3 (econ 4.00, 5w) vs Jersey Aces; 4-0-11-2 (econ 2.75) vs Troy; 4-1-9-3 (econ 2.25) vs MKCC
bowlers.reason: 16 wickets (combined Fnu + Yuvraj profiles). Variable economy — excellent (2.25–2.75) when on, expensive when leaking wides. 5 wides vs Jersey Aces is a red flag for control. Captain should watch his first 2 overs and be ready to rotate him out if he sprays.

bowlers.name: Siddhant Kumar
bowlers.match_data: Season: 10 wkts, econ 5.95. Rested vs Jersey Aces. Last 2: 4-0-12-4 (econ 3.00) vs Troy Titans; 3-1-8-1 (econ 2.67) vs MKCC
bowlers.reason: UPGRADED: Two consecutive excellent spells (Troy Titans + MKCC) earn him 3–4 overs now. Rested vs Jersey Aces — should be fresh and sharp vs Sinclair Lions. Give him his full overs.

bowlers.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
bowlers.reason: Non-negotiable at the death. Slower ball and variation make him hardest to score off in overs 14–16. ALWAYS save his remaining overs for the death.

bowlers.name: Yuvraj Rajpurohit
bowlers.reason: Death specialist when his rhythm is right. The MKCC game (2.25 econ) and Troy Titans (2.75 econ) prove his quality. Pair with Pradhan for a two-pronged death attack.

bowlers.name: Abhinav Tyagi
bowlers.match_data: Season: 8 wkts, econ 6.15. vs Jersey Aces: 2-0-6-1 (econ 3.00) — cheapest spell of his season.
bowlers.reason: Raw pace can surprise at the death. His Jersey Aces spell (3.00 econ) is his best this season — use as a 1-over option if the partnership needs breaking and both Pradhan/Yuvraj overs are done.

economy_first_bowlers.note: These bowlers build pressure through dot balls that lead to wickets for others. Updated after 11 matches.

bowlers.name: Namit Rathi
bowlers.note: Best economy in squad — forces errors through dots; use in PowerPlay every game

bowlers.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
bowlers.note: Economy AND wickets — elite, best in squad by far; one expensive spell (Jersey Aces) is an anomaly

bowlers.name: Malav Shah
bowlers.note: Concedes under run-a-ball — crucial containment role in middle overs

bowlers.name: Yuvraj Rajpurohit
bowlers.note: Good wickets + economy when on — watch for wides, rotate if leaking (5 wides vs Jersey Aces)

bowlers.name: Siddhant Kumar
bowlers.note: Economy variable but has two consecutive excellent spells — now gets 3–4 overs

key_matchups: Win the toss and BAT FIRST — Sinclair Lions (5W/6L) are competitive enough that chasing a low total could be precarious. Post 140+ and set a target WW's bowling can defend.
Ajinkya Pradhan bowls his FULL 4-over quota — 24 wickets at 3.68 is the bedrock. Use 2 overs up front in the PowerPlay, 2 at death. Non-negotiable even after his Jersey Aces blip.
Siddhant Kumar is BACK — rested vs Jersey Aces, expect him fresh. Give him 3–4 overs early in the middle phase. His two-game run of 4-0-12-4 and 3-1-8-1 makes him WW's second-best bowling option.
Vivek Singh MUST bat 6 or 7 — his top-order record (11 runs in 6 innings) versus lower-order record (81 runs in 3 innings incl. 52* off 27) is conclusive. No exceptions, even against weaker teams.
Vinit Keni should bat big — fresh off a 34(14) at SR 243. When he's in this form, the target difference between a 120 total and a 150 total is mostly him. Protect him and let him bat deep.

Watch Yuvraj's wides — 5 wides vs Jersey Aces cost 5 free runs. Against a team with real batters, those will be punished. Captain should pull him after 2 overs if he's spraying, and bring him back when settled.
Amanraj Gupta is a genuine utility asset — use him for 1–2 bowling overs if needed (econ 3.00 vs Jersey Aces). His batting at #7 continues to produce when the game needs finishing.

scorecard_insights.vivek_singh_pattern: Positions 3–4: 5,0,5,0,0,1 = 11 runs in 6 innings. Positions 6–7: 11*,52*,18 = 81 runs in 3 innings. Data is conclusive — he wins games from down the order.
scorecard_insights.vinit_keni_anchor: 208 runs, avg 29.71, SR 116 in 11 games. Last 3 innings: 55*(41), 0(2) vs Troy (low chase), 34(14) SR=243 vs Jersey Aces. He is the unquestioned cornerstone of WW's batting.
scorecard_insights.pradhan_dominance: 11 games, 24 wickets, econ 3.68. His Jersey Aces spell (6.00 econ) is the first time he's gone above 4.50 this season. Still the best bowler WW have — or arguably in Group C.
scorecard_insights.siddhant_breakthrough: Troy Titans: 4-0-12-4 (econ 3.00). MKCC: 3-1-8-1 (econ 2.67). Rested vs Jersey Aces. Two consecutive elite spells + fresh legs = expect big things vs Sinclair Lions.
scorecard_insights.amanraj_finisher: Batting: 19*(11, SR=173) vs Troy, 13*(15) vs MKCC, 5(3) at #3 vs Jersey Aces. Bowling: 2-0-6-1 (econ 3.00) vs Jersey Aces. He is becoming WW's all-round utility player — bat #7, bowl 1–2 overs if needed.
scorecard_insights.rathi_powerplay_weapon: Princeton Walk: 3-1-3-2 (econ 1.00). Jersey Aces: 2-0-5-1 (econ 2.50). Rathi in the PowerPlay is a genuine game-changer. Use him every match he plays.
scorecard_insights.malav_shah_containment: Season: 22 overs, econ 4.05. Most consistent containment bowler in the squad after Pradhan. DNB bowling vs Jersey Aces — not needed in a 15-over bowl-out.
scorecard_insights.yuvraj_wides_warning: 5 wides vs Jersey Aces (4-0-16-3, econ 4.00) — still got 3 wickets but the extras are costly. His merged stats (16 wkts, 4.19 econ) are strong but control must be tighter vs real batting lineups.
scorecard_insights.collapse_warning: Monroe Mavericks (G4): WW collapsed to 21 all out in 9.1 overs. That fragility still lives in this batting order when the top 3 fall early. Protect Keni at all costs — the lower order cannot rescue a 3-wicket collapse without him.
